LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CURTIS CRANE AND BROWNSTEIN & CRANE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE IS OVERRULED AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF ADEQUATELY ALLEGES CLAIMS FOR MEDICAL BATTERY AND PROMISSORY FRAUD. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST THAT THE COURT TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE SPECIAL CONSENT SIGNED ON SEPTEMBER 8, 2015 IS DENIED. THE SPECIAL CONSENT IS NOT EXPLICITLY REFERRED TO IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND IS NOT THE KIND OF DOCUMENT THAT A COURT MAY JUDICIALLY NOTICE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: G. Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Responses To Judicial Council Form Interrogatories (Set No. One) And Special Interrogatories (Set No. One); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Michelle A. Birtja
Off Calendar Apr-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:

Attorney, Markus B. Willoughby of the law offices of Willoughby Law Firm (510-451-2777) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, William B. Barbagallo of the law offices of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP (310-481-7635) specially appears by telephone as counsel for defendant/moving party

Both sides stipulate to judge pro tem. Written stipulation is signed and filed.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides inopen court. The hearing is not reported/transcribed by a certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANT CURTIS CRANE, BROWNSTEIN & CRANE Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Responses To Judicial Council Form Interrogatories (Set No. One) And Special Interrogatories (Set No. One); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Michelle A. Birtja is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS:

1. GRANTED AS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES NOS. 6.4, 6.6 & 8.7 AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS 2, 29, 30 & 33. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO THESE INTERROGATORIES ARE OVERRULED; THE FACT THAT DEFENDANT MIGHT BE ABLE TO OBTAIN THE REQUESTED INFORMATION BY OTHER MEANS DOES NOT EXCUSE PLAINTIFF FROM MAKING A REASONABLE AND GOOD FAITH EFFORT TO OBTAIN THE REQUESTED INFORMATION. PLAINTIFF WILL DELIVER HIS FURTHER RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION WITHIN THIRTY (30) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. Provide specificity as to dates and income lost.

2. DENIED AS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES NOS. 6.7, 8.8, 12.1 & 12.6 AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 1,6, 7, 8, 25, 26 & 31.

3. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED.

Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul A. Renne: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (DISC302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michelle A. Birtja, Esq. of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP, T:310.481.7618, for defendant and moving party, by Court Call;
Markus B. Willoughby, Esq. of Willoughby Law Firm, T:510.451.2777, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CURTIS CRANE and BROWNSTEIN & CRANE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to both causes of action alleged in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff John Doe is OVERRULED as to both causes of action. Plaintiff adequately alleges claims for medical battery and promissory fraud. Defendants' request that the court take judicial notice of the special consent signed on September 8, 2015 is DENIED. The special consent is not explicitly referred to in the first amended complaint and is not the kind of document that a court may judicially notice.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Giselle Casey CSR#8090, email: casey.giselle@gmail.com
